description_long: This is a tall sunflower with a single, large, round flower head featuring a dense array of yellow petals radiating outward from a dark brown central disc. The flower is supported by a sturdy, green stem that is straight and upright, with several broad, ovate leaves branching off at intervals along its length. The leaves are a medium green color, with visible veins and slightly serrated edges, and are arranged alternately on the stem. The flower head is prominent and faces forward, displaying a radial symmetry typical of sunflowers. The back of the flower head shows green bracts that support the petals. This plant is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, and roadside plantings, adding a vibrant touch of nature to city environments.
description: A tall sunflower with a large yellow flower head, broad green leaves, and a sturdy green stem. The flower displays radial symmetry and is commonly seen in urban gardens and parks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the sunflower's profile, with the flower head facing right and the stem slightly curved. Several leaves are visible along the stem.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full face of the sunflower, with the yellow petals radiating from the dark central disc and leaves symmetrically arranged on either side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the flower head facing left and the leaves visible along the stem.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the flower head with green bracts and the arrangement of leaves along the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial pattern around the central disc, forming a single whorl with overlapping and slightly layered edges, exhibiting strong radial symmetry typical of the Asteraceae family.
